---
# AF-2026-05-31-1 / Test12 F-05 — brownfield-assessment template.
#
# The brownfield SKILL.md Phase 1 prose used to say "read the assessment
# template" without shipping one. This template fills that gap so the Phase 1
# discovery subagent (and any human reader) has a concrete shape to fill in.
#
# Generated by: /gaia-brownfield Phase 1 (discovery)
# Consumed by:  /gaia-brownfield Phase 2 (documentation subagents),
#               /gaia-brownfield Phase 7 (gap consolidation),
#               /gaia-brownfield Phase 8 (PRD generation).
#
# This frontmatter MUST be present on the rendered artifact. The brownfield
# orchestrator stamps it; the Phase 7 consolidator scrapes it.
mode: brownfield
# AF-2026-05-31-3 / Test14 F-10: schema_version 2.0.0 is the canonical
# value documented by the first-30-minutes-brownfield tutorial Step 7
# ("If you see anything else, run /gaia-migrate v1 v2"). The prior
# "1.0" template-default contradicted the downstream contract.
schema_version: "2.0.0"
# AF-2026-06-01-1 / Test15 F-07: project_type drives Phase-8a PRD
# template selection (application | infrastructure | platform — see
# the brownfield SKILL Phase 8a table) and Phase-7 consolidation. The
# Phase-1 SKILL prose says "Include {project_type} in the output" but
# the shipped template never carried the placeholder, so the Phase-2
# subagent had to hand-inject it. Default is `application` since most
# greenfield → brownfield transitions are application repos; override
# in the discovery questionnaire when the target is infrastructure
# (Terraform/Pulumi) or a platform (multi-tenant control plane).
project_type: "{application|infrastructure|platform}"
generated_by: /gaia-brownfield
generated_at: "{YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ}"
project_path: "{absolute path to scanned codebase}"
scan_fidelity: "{tier-0 | tier-1 | tier-2}"   # populated by gaia-doctor probe
---

# Brownfield Assessment — {project name}

## 1. Project Overview

One-paragraph plain-language summary of what the project does, who built it,
and where it sits in the org. Pulled from README headers + top-level docs by
the Phase 1 discovery subagent; refined by the operator before Phase 2.

- **Domain:** {e.g., "internal CLI for vault operations", "B2B SaaS billing"}.
- **Primary language(s):** {python, go, typescript, ...}.
- **Build / package manager:** {pyproject.toml + uv, package.json + npm, ...}.
- **Test framework(s) detected:** {pytest, jest, go test, ...}.
- **Deployment shape:** {library, single-backend service, microservices, ...}.

## 2. Repository Layout

Top-level directory tree with one-line annotations. Mark which paths are
in-scope for the GAIA lifecycle (`✓`) vs out-of-scope vendored content (`–`):

```
.
├── core/             ✓ application source
├── tests/            ✓ test suite
├── scripts/          ✓ build + ops tooling
├── vendor/           – vendored third-party (excluded from scan)
├── docs/             ✓ existing documentation
└── .gaia/            ✓ GAIA runtime tree (created by /gaia-init)
```

## 3. Existing Documentation Surface

What docs already exist, where they live, when they were last touched, and
whether they are likely to be authoritative or stale.

| Path | Purpose | Last touched | Authoritative? |
| ---- | ------- | ------------ | -------------- |
| `README.md` | project intro + run instructions | YYYY-MM-DD | ✓ |
| `docs/architecture.md` | system overview | YYYY-MM-DD | partial — Phase 2 will verify |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | dev setup | YYYY-MM-DD | ? |

## 4. Stack Signals (raw)

The deterministic signal scan from `detect-signals.sh` — manifest filenames
present, ecosystem fingerprints, multi-stack partitioning (if applicable):

- `pyproject.toml` at `./core/` → ecosystem: python
- `go.mod` at `./internal/cli/` → ecosystem: go
- `package.json` at `./web-ui/` → ecosystem: node

`stacks_proposed`: {N}    `auto_detected_paths`: {y|n}    `mode`: {proposal|audit|single-stack}

## 5. Known Gaps (operator-asserted before scans run)

Anything the operator already knows is missing or weak — populated from the
intake questionnaire, used to seed Phase 3 scan focus:

- [ ] No CI pipeline configured.
- [ ] Test coverage unmeasured / unknown.
- [ ] Dependency lock files out of date.
- [ ] No threat model documented.

## 6. Scan-readiness checklist

| Check | Status | Notes |
| ----- | ------ | ----- |
| `bash` ≥ 4.0 available | {✓ \| ✗} | macOS default is 3.2 — install via `brew install bash` |
| `grype` on PATH | {✓ \| ✗} | for CVE scan (Phase 3) |
| `syft` on PATH | {✓ \| ✗} | for SBOM production (Phase 3) |
| `cdxgen` on PATH | {✓ \| ✗} | for completeness check (Phase 3) |
| Docker available | {✓ \| ✗} | enables the `gaia-tools` runner cascade |
| `gaia-doctor` verdict | {tier-0 \| tier-1 \| tier-2} | run before Phase 3 |

## 7. Continuation pointer

After this assessment is signed off, the brownfield orchestrator moves to:

- **Phase 2** — parallel documentation subagents (architecture, deps, tests,
  env, NFRs).
- **Phase 3** — deep-scan subagents (doc-code drift, hardcoded values,
  integration seams, runtime behavior, security, config contradictions, dead
  code) consuming this assessment + Phase 2 output.

Frontmatter `mode: brownfield` is what flips downstream gates (e.g.
create-epics SV-20 advisory mode per AF-2026-05-31-1 / Test12 F-14).
